destinations.evidence: The HTTP async precompile decoder uses the ABI request type and returns the executor field from the encoded request head.
Payload detection rejects malformed/too-short ABI through validation/decoding and propagates the decoded executor into the generated async commitment.

equivalence_argument: The current detector path validates the async payload and decodes the executor through the ABI codec; malformed or too-short inputs fail before an executor address can be propagated.
rationale: The commit title and code comments say the HTTPCallRequest is ABI encoded with the executor address as the first tuple field, and the old RLP extraction is replaced by alloy-sol-types address decoding over the first 32 bytes. The extracted address is the required executor_address passed into AsyncCallResult and the async-exec transaction, so decoding it according to the request ABI is externally observable routing behavior. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: For an HTTP async precompile input whose first ABI-encoded field is an address, async call detection must decode that first 32-byte ABI word as the executor address and propagate it into the AsyncCallResult/system async-exec transaction; inputs shorter than one ABI word must be rejected instead of producing an executor address.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct an HTTP_CALL_PRECOMPILE transaction input whose first ABI word encodes executor address A and whose remaining ABI tuple data is valid dynamic request payload, run the async detector path that creates AsyncCallResult, and assert result.executor_address and the generated TxAsyncExec executor_address are A. Also pass an input shorter than 32 bytes and assert detection fails instead of returning a default or truncated address.
summary: HTTP async executor addresses come from the ABI request field and are propagated to the system transaction.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create an ABI HTTPCallRequest whose executor is A and assert build_short_async_commitment_transaction produces a commitment with executor_address A; pass fewer than 32 bytes and assert decoding fails.
title: Executor address comes from ABI request head
